title: Day 2 Vocabulary — Self Introduction (Complete Edition)
description: Complete vocabulary for Day 2: Self Introduction — covering core introduction verbs, adjectives, professional terms, hobbies, family, education, interview vocabulary, business terms, and advanced professional expressions.

words.simpleMeaning: to present yourself or someone to others

sentences.daily: Let me introduce myself to the group.
sentences.office: I will introduce the new team member tomorrow.
sentences.interview: Please introduce yourself briefly.
sentences.business: Allow me to introduce our company's vision.
sentences.formal: It is my pleasure to introduce our keynote speaker.
sentences.informal: Hey, let me introduce you to my friend.

words.usageNote: Most formal way to start a self-introduction.

words.simpleMeaning: to say hello or welcome someone

sentences.daily: I greet my neighbors every morning.
sentences.office: Always greet your clients with a smile.
sentences.interview: The interviewer greeted me warmly.
sentences.business: We greet every customer at the door.
sentences.formal: Please allow me to greet you on behalf of our organization.
sentences.informal: Hey! Greet your friends properly.

words.usageNote: First step in any introduction or conversation.

words.simpleMeaning: to say or write something briefly

sentences.daily: Please mention your hobbies in the form.
sentences.office: Mention your achievements in the report.
sentences.interview: Mention any relevant experience you have.
sentences.business: Always mention your company name first.
sentences.formal: I would like to mention a few key points.
sentences.informal: Did you mention where you are from?

words.usageNote: Used to bring up specific facts during introduction.

words.simpleMeaning: to explain what someone or something is like

sentences.daily: Describe yourself in three words.
sentences.office: Describe your role in the last project.
sentences.interview: Can you describe your biggest achievement?
sentences.business: Please describe your product's main features.
sentences.formal: The candidate was asked to describe their background.
sentences.informal: Describe your hometown to me!

words.usageNote: Key verb for talking about yourself in detail.

words.simpleMeaning: to tell others about something; to have something in common

sentences.daily: Share your name and hometown with the class.
sentences.office: Please share your work experience.
sentences.interview: I would like to share something important about myself.
sentences.business: Let me share my professional background.
sentences.formal: The speaker shared valuable insights about his journey.
sentences.informal: Share your story with us!

words.usageNote: Natural verb for informal introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: to show, give, or introduce formally

sentences.daily: Present yourself confidently in any situation.
sentences.office: He was asked to present his ideas to the team.
sentences.interview: Present yourself professionally during the interview.
sentences.business: Allow me to present our company profile.
sentences.formal: The chairperson was presented to the board.
sentences.informal: Present your best self!

words.usageNote: Formal alternative to introduce.

words.simpleMeaning: to be from a place or part of a group

sentences.daily: I belong to a small town in Rajasthan.
sentences.office: She belongs to the marketing department.
sentences.interview: Where do you belong to originally?
sentences.business: Our company belongs to the technology sector.
sentences.formal: The artifact belongs to the 18th century.
sentences.informal: I belong to a big family of five.

words.usageNote: Used to state origin or membership.

words.simpleMeaning: to come from a particular place or beginning

sentences.daily: My family originates from Gujarat.
sentences.office: This idea originates from our R&D team.
sentences.interview: Where did your interest in coding originate?
sentences.business: The company originated as a small startup.
sentences.formal: The tradition originated over 500 years ago.
sentences.informal: So where does your family originate from?

words.usageNote: More formal way of saying "come from".

words.simpleMeaning: to come from a particular place

sentences.daily: I hail from a small village in Uttar Pradesh.
sentences.office: Our CEO hails from a humble background.
sentences.interview: The candidate hails from Bengaluru.
sentences.business: Our founders hail from the finance industry.
sentences.formal: The distinguished guest hails from London.
sentences.informal: Where do you hail from?

words.usageNote: Elegant way to express place of origin.

words.simpleMeaning: to live in a particular place

sentences.daily: I currently reside in Mumbai.
sentences.office: The client resides in the United States.
sentences.interview: Where do you currently reside?
sentences.business: Our headquarters resides in New Delhi.
sentences.formal: The ambassador resides in the capital city.
sentences.informal: Where do you reside these days?

words.usageNote: Formal word for "live in a place".

words.simpleMeaning: to work toward a goal or continue an activity

sentences.daily: I am pursuing my dream of becoming a writer.
sentences.office: She is pursuing a career in data science.
sentences.interview: I am pursuing a certification in project management.
sentences.business: We are pursuing new market opportunities.
sentences.formal: He pursued his academic goals with great dedication.
sentences.informal: What goals are you pursuing right now?

words.usageNote: Important verb when talking about career/education goals.

words.simpleMeaning: to want to achieve something great

sentences.daily: I aspire to become a successful entrepreneur.
sentences.office: She aspires to lead the entire department.
sentences.interview: I aspire to contribute meaningfully to this company.
sentences.business: We aspire to become the market leader.
sentences.formal: The young graduate aspired to change the world.
sentences.informal: What do you aspire to become?

words.usageNote: Shows ambition in professional introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: to focus on one particular area

sentences.daily: I specialize in graphic design.
sentences.office: Our team specializes in software development.
sentences.interview: I specialize in customer relationship management.
sentences.business: We specialize in cloud computing solutions.
sentences.formal: The doctor specializes in pediatric care.
sentences.informal: I specialize in cooking South Indian food.

words.usageNote: Shows expertise in self-introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: to give or add something valuable

sentences.daily: I want to contribute to my community.
sentences.office: He contributes new ideas to every project.
sentences.interview: I aim to contribute my skills to your team.
sentences.business: Our company contributes to sustainable development.
sentences.formal: Each member is expected to contribute to the team's success.
sentences.informal: What can you contribute to our group?

words.usageNote: Strong word for interviews — shows value.

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully complete or achieve something

sentences.daily: I have accomplished many personal goals this year.
sentences.office: She accomplished the project ahead of schedule.
sentences.interview: What have you accomplished in your last role?
sentences.business: Our team accomplished record-breaking sales.
sentences.formal: He has accomplished remarkable feats in his career.
sentences.informal: Look what I accomplished today!

words.usageNote: Key word for talking about achievements.

words.simpleMeaning: to handle or be in charge of something

sentences.daily: I manage my time effectively.
sentences.office: She manages a team of 20 people.
sentences.interview: I have managed projects worth over 1 crore rupees.
sentences.business: He manages all client relationships.
sentences.formal: The executive manages the entire Eastern division.
sentences.informal: Can you manage this task on your own?

words.usageNote: Shows leadership skill in introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: to guide or be in charge of a group

sentences.daily: I lead a reading group in my neighborhood.
sentences.office: She leads the product development team.
sentences.interview: I have led cross-functional teams of 15 members.
sentences.business: He leads the company's innovation strategy.
sentences.formal: The general was chosen to lead the mission.
sentences.informal: Who leads your group of friends?

words.usageNote: Powerful verb for leadership roles.

words.simpleMeaning: to build or improve something over time

sentences.daily: I am developing my public speaking skills.
sentences.office: We are developing a new mobile application.
sentences.interview: I developed strong communication skills at my last job.
sentences.business: Our company develops enterprise software solutions.
sentences.formal: The researcher has developed a groundbreaking treatment.
sentences.informal: I am developing a habit of reading every day.

words.usageNote: Shows growth mindset in introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully reach a goal or standard

sentences.daily: I achieved my fitness goal this year.
sentences.office: She achieved the highest sales record.
sentences.interview: I aim to achieve excellence in everything I do.
sentences.business: Our startup achieved profitability within one year.
sentences.formal: The scientist achieved a major breakthrough.
sentences.informal: What have you achieved recently?

words.usageNote: Essential for discussing accomplishments.

words.simpleMeaning: to work together with others

sentences.daily: I love to collaborate with creative people.
sentences.office: We regularly collaborate with other departments.
sentences.interview: I thrive when I collaborate with diverse teams.
sentences.business: Our company collaborates with international partners.
sentences.formal: The two universities collaborated on the research project.
sentences.informal: Let's collaborate on this assignment!

words.usageNote: Modern professional word showing teamwork skills.

words.simpleMeaning: to share information or ideas with others

sentences.daily: I communicate clearly in all situations.
sentences.office: She communicates effectively with all stakeholders.
sentences.interview: I am known for my ability to communicate complex ideas simply.
sentences.business: We communicate our brand values through every product.
sentences.formal: The CEO communicates the company's vision to shareholders.
sentences.informal: We need to communicate better as a team.

words.usageNote: Top soft skill for professional introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: to build professional relationships

sentences.daily: I like to network at community events.
sentences.office: She networks actively with industry professionals.
sentences.interview: I network regularly on LinkedIn and at conferences.
sentences.business: Networking helped us win our biggest client.
sentences.formal: The executive networks with global business leaders.
sentences.informal: Let's network and exchange contact details.

words.usageNote: Essential in professional self-introduction contexts.

words.simpleMeaning: to be very good at something

sentences.daily: I excel at solving complex problems.
sentences.office: She excels in data analysis.
sentences.interview: I excel when working under pressure.
sentences.business: Our team excels at delivering on time.
sentences.formal: The student excelled in all academic disciplines.
sentences.informal: You really excel at this!

words.usageNote: Shows skill and confidence.

words.simpleMeaning: to change or adjust to new situations

sentences.daily: I adapt quickly to new environments.
sentences.office: She adapted smoothly to the new work culture.
sentences.interview: I can adapt to different work styles and team dynamics.
sentences.business: Our company adapted its strategy during the pandemic.
sentences.formal: The organization adapted its processes efficiently.
sentences.informal: Can you adapt to our team's schedule?

words.usageNote: Crucial adaptability skill for interviews.

words.simpleMeaning: to create new ideas or methods

sentences.daily: I always look for ways to innovate in my work.
sentences.office: Our department is constantly innovating.
sentences.interview: I am passionate about innovating solutions to real problems.
sentences.business: We innovate to stay ahead of the competition.
sentences.formal: The team innovated a cost-saving manufacturing process.
sentences.informal: Let's innovate and try something new!

words.usageNote: Highly valued word in modern professional contexts.

words.simpleMeaning: to encourage someone to take action

sentences.daily: I motivate myself by setting small daily goals.
sentences.office: She motivates her team through positive reinforcement.
sentences.interview: What motivates you to go above and beyond?
sentences.business: Our mission motivates every decision we make.
sentences.formal: The principal motivates students with monthly awards.
sentences.informal: You really motivate me to do better!

words.usageNote: Common interview question word.

words.simpleMeaning: to give full effort or attention to something

sentences.daily: I dedicate two hours daily to learning English.
sentences.office: She dedicates herself entirely to her work.
sentences.interview: I am dedicated to continuous professional growth.
sentences.business: We dedicate our resources to customer satisfaction.
sentences.formal: He dedicated his career to public service.
sentences.informal: I dedicate this win to my entire team!

words.usageNote: Shows commitment and work ethic.

words.simpleMeaning: to involve yourself or others in an activity

sentences.daily: I engage in community service every weekend.
sentences.office: She engages clients with her friendly personality.
sentences.interview: I actively engage in professional development activities.
sentences.business: We engage our customers through social media.
sentences.formal: The professor engages students with real-world examples.
sentences.informal: Engage yourself in something productive today.

words.usageNote: Shows participation and social involvement.

words.simpleMeaning: to grow or succeed very well

sentences.daily: I thrive in fast-paced environments.
sentences.office: She thrives when given creative freedom.
sentences.interview: I thrive when collaborating with talented people.
sentences.business: Our company thrives because of customer loyalty.
sentences.formal: The organization thrives under strong leadership.
sentences.informal: I thrive when I have a clear goal to chase.

words.usageNote: Powerful self-description word for interviews.

words.simpleMeaning: to keep equal importance between different things

sentences.daily: I balance work and personal life well.
sentences.office: She balances multiple projects simultaneously.
sentences.interview: I know how to balance deadlines and quality.
sentences.business: We balance innovation with practicality.
sentences.formal: The manager balanced team needs with company goals.
sentences.informal: I try to balance fun and responsibility.

words.usageNote: Important for work-life balance discussions.

words.simpleMeaning: to exchange information with others effectively

sentences.daily: I communicate my ideas clearly to everyone.
sentences.office: He communicates project updates to all stakeholders.
sentences.interview: I communicate professionally both verbally and in writing.
sentences.business: We communicate our values to every customer.
sentences.formal: The board communicates decisions through official memos.
sentences.informal: Talk to me! We need to communicate more.

words.usageNote: Core competency word.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something more powerful or effective

sentences.daily: I strengthen my English skills every day.
sentences.office: She is strengthening the team's technical expertise.
sentences.interview: I look to strengthen my leadership skills in this role.
sentences.business: We are strengthening our market position.
sentences.formal: The training program strengthens core competencies.
sentences.informal: Work hard to strengthen your weak areas.

words.usageNote: Shows growth orientation.

words.simpleMeaning: to grow larger or broader

sentences.daily: I am expanding my skill set this year.
sentences.office: We are expanding into new markets.
sentences.interview: I want to expand my professional network through this role.
sentences.business: Our company is expanding its product line.
sentences.formal: The university is expanding its research programs.
sentences.informal: Let's expand our horizons together!

words.usageNote: Career growth word.

words.simpleMeaning: to change completely

sentences.daily: I transformed my life through hard work.
sentences.office: She transformed the company's culture.
sentences.interview: I aim to transform challenges into opportunities.
sentences.business: We transform data into actionable insights.
sentences.formal: The leader transformed the organization's vision.
sentences.informal: You have transformed so much this year!

words.usageNote: Impactful word for personal growth stories.

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully deal with a problem or difficulty

sentences.daily: I overcame my fear of public speaking.
sentences.office: She overcame every obstacle in the project.
sentences.interview: Tell me about a challenge you overcame.
sentences.business: We overcame supply chain issues through innovation.
sentences.formal: The team overcame significant technical difficulties.
sentences.informal: You can overcome anything you set your mind to.

words.usageNote: STAR method interview answer word.

words.simpleMeaning: to show evidence of something

sentences.daily: I demonstrate my commitment through consistent effort.
sentences.office: She demonstrates exceptional leadership every day.
sentences.interview: I have demonstrated strong analytical skills in my previous roles.
sentences.business: We demonstrate our value through measurable results.
sentences.formal: The candidate demonstrated mastery of the required skills.
sentences.informal: Let me demonstrate how to do this step by step.

words.usageNote: Evidence-based vocabulary for interviews.

words.simpleMeaning: to think deeply about something; to show something

sentences.daily: I reflect on my day every evening.
sentences.office: Her work reflects excellent attention to detail.
sentences.interview: I regularly reflect on my strengths and areas of improvement.
sentences.business: Our service reflects our commitment to quality.
sentences.formal: The report reflects the team's collective effort.
sentences.informal: Take time to reflect on your goals.

words.usageNote: Growth mindset vocabulary.

words.simpleMeaning: to make practical or effective use of something

sentences.daily: I utilize every opportunity to learn.
sentences.office: She utilizes data to make better decisions.
sentences.interview: I know how to utilize available resources efficiently.
sentences.business: We utilize cutting-edge technology in our operations.
sentences.formal: The researcher utilized advanced methods in the study.
sentences.informal: Utilize your time wisely every day.

words.usageNote: Professional alternative to "use".

words.simpleMeaning: to organize different parts to work together

sentences.daily: I coordinate tasks for my study group.
sentences.office: She coordinates all client meetings.
sentences.interview: I have experience coordinating large-scale events.
sentences.business: We coordinate with vendors across three countries.
sentences.formal: The project manager coordinates every phase of the project.
sentences.informal: Who coordinates the schedule for our group?

words.usageNote: Project management vocabulary.

words.simpleMeaning: to decide what is most important first

sentences.daily: I prioritize tasks based on deadlines and importance.
sentences.office: She always prioritizes customer needs.
sentences.interview: I know how to prioritize under pressure.
sentences.business: We prioritize quality over speed.
sentences.formal: The committee prioritized infrastructure projects.
sentences.informal: You need to prioritize your health above everything.

words.usageNote: Time management word.

words.simpleMeaning: having very strong feelings about something

sentences.daily: I am passionate about learning new languages.
sentences.office: She is passionate about user experience design.
sentences.interview: I am passionate about solving real-world problems.
sentences.business: We are passionate about delivering value to customers.
sentences.formal: The researcher is passionate about climate science.
sentences.informal: I am so passionate about this topic!

words.usageNote: Top personality word for introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: giving full effort to something

sentences.daily: I am a dedicated learner.
sentences.office: She is a dedicated employee who never misses deadlines.
sentences.interview: I am highly dedicated to professional excellence.
sentences.business: Our team is dedicated to client satisfaction.
sentences.formal: The scientist was a dedicated researcher for 20 years.
sentences.informal: You are one of the most dedicated people I know.

words.usageNote: Shows strong work ethic.

words.simpleMeaning: willing to work with great effort

sentences.daily: I am a hardworking student.
sentences.office: She is recognized as the most hardworking person on the team.
sentences.interview: I am hardworking and always deliver results on time.
sentences.business: We are hardworking professionals who go the extra mile.
sentences.formal: The organization values hardworking individuals.
sentences.informal: Everyone knows how hardworking you are!

words.usageNote: Classic positive trait in introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: having great excitement and interest

sentences.daily: I am enthusiastic about learning every day.
sentences.office: She brings an enthusiastic energy to every meeting.
sentences.interview: I am enthusiastic about this opportunity to join your company.
sentences.business: We are enthusiastic about our new product launch.
sentences.formal: The conference was attended by enthusiastic participants.
sentences.informal: You seem really enthusiastic about this idea!

words.usageNote: Energy and positivity word.

words.simpleMeaning: can be trusted to do what is expected

sentences.daily: I am a reliable person — my friends always count on me.
sentences.office: She is the most reliable member of the department.
sentences.interview: I am reliable and consistently meet all deadlines.
sentences.business: Our company is known for being a reliable partner.
sentences.formal: The system is reliable under all operating conditions.
sentences.informal: You can always count on me — I am reliable!

words.usageNote: Trust-building trait.

words.simpleMeaning: having the ability to think of new ideas

sentences.daily: I am a creative person who loves arts and music.
sentences.office: She brings creative solutions to complex problems.
sentences.interview: I am creative and bring fresh perspectives to challenges.
sentences.business: Our marketing team is highly creative.
sentences.formal: The artist is widely recognized for his creative brilliance.
sentences.informal: You are so creative — love your ideas!

words.usageNote: Valued in many modern job roles.

words.simpleMeaning: good at carefully examining information

sentences.daily: I have an analytical mind — I love solving puzzles.
sentences.office: She takes an analytical approach to problem-solving.
sentences.interview: I am an analytical thinker with attention to detail.
sentences.business: We use analytical methods to make business decisions.
sentences.formal: The analyst used an analytical framework for the study.
sentences.informal: You always think so analytically!

words.usageNote: Strong for technical/analytical roles.

words.simpleMeaning: acting in advance to deal with something

sentences.daily: I am proactive in identifying opportunities.
sentences.office: She takes a proactive approach to customer service.
sentences.interview: I am proactive and take initiative without being told.
sentences.business: We are proactive in anticipating market changes.
sentences.formal: The committee took a proactive stance on the issue.
sentences.informal: Be proactive — don't wait for things to happen!

words.usageNote: Top professional trait.

words.simpleMeaning: able to do many different things well

sentences.daily: I am a versatile person with many interests.
sentences.office: She is a versatile employee who handles multiple roles.
sentences.interview: I am versatile and can work across different domains.
sentences.business: Our platform is versatile and serves multiple industries.
sentences.formal: The professor is versatile in both research and teaching.
sentences.informal: You are so versatile — you can do anything!

words.usageNote: Multi-skill word for professionals.

words.simpleMeaning: having a strong desire to succeed

sentences.daily: I am ambitious and always aim for the top.
sentences.office: She is an ambitious professional climbing the corporate ladder.
sentences.interview: I am ambitious and set high standards for myself.
sentences.business: Our founders are ambitious visionaries.
sentences.formal: The ambitious project was completed ahead of schedule.
sentences.informal: You are so ambitious — never stop chasing your dreams!

words.usageNote: Shows drive and determination.

words.simpleMeaning: able to speak or write a language easily and accurately

sentences.daily: I am fluent in both Hindi and English.
sentences.office: She is fluent in three languages.
sentences.interview: I am fluent in English and can handle business communication.
sentences.business: We need a fluent English speaker for the client role.
sentences.formal: The candidate demonstrated fluent command of the language.
sentences.informal: Your English is so fluent now!

words.usageNote: Key language skill word.

words.simpleMeaning: very skilled and competent at something

sentences.daily: I am proficient in Microsoft Office tools.
sentences.office: She is proficient in Python and JavaScript.
sentences.interview: I am proficient in data analysis and reporting.
sentences.business: We need someone proficient in project management.
sentences.formal: The employee is proficient in all required technical skills.
sentences.informal: You have become so proficient at this!

words.usageNote: Skill competency word.

words.simpleMeaning: having the required ability or skills

sentences.daily: I consider myself a competent problem-solver.
sentences.office: She is a highly competent project manager.
sentences.interview: I am a competent professional with 5 years of experience.
sentences.business: Our team is competent in handling complex client needs.
sentences.formal: The board selected the most competent candidate.
sentences.informal: You are more competent than you think!

words.usageNote: Professional qualification word.

words.simpleMeaning: having knowledge or skill gained over time

sentences.daily: I am an experienced driver.
sentences.office: She is an experienced software architect.
sentences.interview: I am an experienced professional with a strong track record.
sentences.business: We hire only experienced consultants.
sentences.formal: The experienced researcher led the study.
sentences.informal: You are really experienced in this area.

words.usageNote: Credibility builder in introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: having the right education or experience

sentences.daily: I am qualified to teach English.
sentences.office: She is a qualified chartered accountant.
sentences.interview: I am highly qualified for this position.
sentences.business: We only work with qualified professionals.
sentences.formal: The qualified engineer designed the new structure.
sentences.informal: Are you qualified to handle this task?

words.usageNote: Formal credential word.

words.simpleMeaning: having official recognition of a skill or knowledge

sentences.daily: I am a certified yoga instructor.
sentences.office: She is a certified PMP project manager.
sentences.interview: I am certified in Google Analytics and Digital Marketing.
sentences.business: Our engineers are certified by industry bodies.
sentences.formal: The certified public accountant filed the company's taxes.
sentences.informal: I just became certified — I passed the exam!

words.usageNote: Shows formal credentials.

words.simpleMeaning: having particular abilities and expertise

sentences.daily: I am skilled at cooking and baking.
sentences.office: She is a highly skilled data scientist.
sentences.interview: I am a skilled communicator and team player.
sentences.business: We seek skilled professionals to join our firm.
sentences.formal: The skilled surgeon performed the operation successfully.
sentences.informal: You are so skilled at everything you do!

words.usageNote: General competency word.

words.simpleMeaning: having a natural ability to do something well

sentences.daily: I am a talented artist and painter.
sentences.office: She is the most talented designer on our team.
sentences.interview: I am a talented individual with a diverse skill set.
sentences.business: We are proud to have talented professionals on our team.
sentences.formal: The talented musician performed at the concert.
sentences.informal: You are so talented — never stop creating!

words.usageNote: Positive personality word.

words.simpleMeaning: highly motivated to succeed

sentences.daily: I am a driven individual who always seeks to improve.
sentences.office: She is a driven professional who consistently exceeds targets.
sentences.interview: I am driven by the desire to create meaningful impact.
sentences.business: Our team is driven by customer success.
sentences.formal: The driven entrepreneur founded three companies by age 30.
sentences.informal: You are such a driven person — I admire that!

words.usageNote: Shows internal motivation.

words.simpleMeaning: directing all attention to one thing

sentences.daily: I stay focused on my goals every single day.
sentences.office: She remains focused even during high-pressure situations.
sentences.interview: I am focused on continuous learning and growth.
sentences.business: We are focused on delivering results that matter.
sentences.formal: The surgeon remained focused throughout the procedure.
sentences.informal: Stay focused — you are almost there!

words.usageNote: Concentration and discipline word.

words.simpleMeaning: information about a person's past

sentences.daily: My background is in arts and literature.
sentences.office: Can you share your professional background?
sentences.interview: My background includes 5 years in IT management.
sentences.business: Our company has a strong background in logistics.
sentences.formal: The applicant's background was thoroughly reviewed.
sentences.informal: Tell me about your background!

words.usageNote: Key self-introduction word.

words.simpleMeaning: knowledge or skill from doing something

sentences.daily: I have 3 years of experience in teaching.
sentences.office: She has vast experience in project management.
sentences.interview: My experience includes leading teams of 20+ people.
sentences.business: Our team brings 50 years of combined experience.
sentences.formal: The professor has extensive research experience.
sentences.informal: What experience do you have with this?

words.usageNote: Most important word in professional introductions.

words.simpleMeaning: an official record of achieving a standard

sentences.daily: I have a Bachelor's degree as my main qualification.
sentences.office: What qualifications are required for this role?
sentences.interview: My qualifications include an MBA from a top university.
sentences.business: We verify all professional qualifications before hiring.
sentences.formal: The candidate's qualifications meet the required standards.
sentences.informal: Do you have the qualifications for this job?

words.usageNote: Academic and professional credentials.

words.simpleMeaning: something accomplished successfully

sentences.daily: Finishing this course is a major achievement.
sentences.office: Her greatest achievement was launching the product on time.
sentences.interview: My proudest achievement is building a team from scratch.
sentences.business: We celebrate every team achievement publicly.
sentences.formal: The award recognizes lifetime achievement in science.
sentences.informal: That is a huge achievement — well done!

words.usageNote: Key accomplishment word for interviews.

words.simpleMeaning: a positive quality or skill

sentences.daily: My greatest strength is my ability to stay calm under pressure.
sentences.office: Her strengths include leadership and communication.
sentences.interview: My top strength is solving complex problems analytically.
sentences.business: Our company's strength is its loyal customer base.
sentences.formal: The candidate demonstrated clear strengths in all assessed areas.
sentences.informal: What do you think your biggest strength is?
